backend/main.mo: Motoko actor that defines a Match type and exposes a query method `getMatches` returning a hardcoded list of 4 matches with Solo/Duo game modes, entry fees, prize pools, and statuses. Also includes blob storage mixin.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report listing two implemented features: a RegistrationModal (collects in-game name, player ID, WhatsApp number) and a UPI Payment screen (shows UPI ID, screenshot upload), both following the dark neon-red theme.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-sentence summaries for each frontend source file, serving as a reference index for the project's component and hook structure.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind base CSS file defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark theme tokens including background, card, border, and chart colors.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the Elite Gaming Hub app, mounting the React app at `#root` and loading `src/main.tsx` as a module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component composing the fixed Header, TournamentBanner, MatchList, and a footer with copyright and caffeine.ai attribution.
frontend/src/components/ConfirmationModa: Success dialog modal shown after payment submission, displaying a check icon, match details (game mode, entry fee, prize pool), and the player's registration info summary.
frontend/src/components/Header.tsx: Fixed top navigation header rendering the Elite Gaming Hub logo with neon red Orbitron styling and a circular profile avatar button with neon red border glow.
frontend/src/components/MatchCard.tsx: Match card component that manages the multi-step registration flow state (idle → registration → payment → confirmation), rendering the card UI and conditionally opening RegistrationModal, PaymentModal, and ConfirmationModal.
frontend/src/components/MatchList.tsx: Displays a grid of live match cards with loading skeleton, error, and empty states, fetching data via the useMatches hook with a refresh button.
frontend/src/components/MatchListSkeleto: Renders a 4-card skeleton placeholder grid mimicking the MatchCard layout for use during loading states.
frontend/src/components/PaymentModal.tsx: A dialog modal for submitting payment for a match, allowing users to copy a UPI ID and upload a payment screenshot before confirming.
frontend/src/components/RegistrationModa: A dialog modal form that collects a player's in-game name, player ID, and WhatsApp number with validation before submitting registration data.
frontend/src/components/TournamentBanner: An auto-advancing image carousel banner showcasing tournament slides with title, prize pool, and navigation controls, cycling every 5 seconds.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: A React Query hook that creates and caches an ICP backend actor, using an authenticated or anonymous identity, and invalidates dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an Internet Identity authentication context and provider, exposing login/logout functions, identity state, and login status via a React context and hook.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Exports a useMatches hook that fetches the list of matches from the ICP backend actor using React Query, with 30-second stale time and 60-second refetch interval.
frontend/src/index.css: Global CSS file that imports Rajdhani and Orbitron fonts, sets up Tailwind layers, defines CSS custom properties for a dark neon-red theme, and adds utility classes for neon glow effects, shimmer, and slide-in animations.
frontend/src/main.tsx: React app entry point that wraps the App component with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider, then mounts it to the DOM root.
frontend/src/types/registration.ts: TypeScript type definitions for the tournament registration flow, including RegistrationData, PaymentData, and RegistrationFlowState interfaces with step tracking.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the theme with Orbitron/Rajdhani fonts, neon-red color palette using oklch values, custom box shadows, and accordion keyframe animations, with typography, container-queries, and animate plugins.
project_state.json: Project state tracking file describing the Elite Gaming Hub Free Fire tournament app, its dark neon-red theme, existing features (header, banner, match cards, registration flow), architectural notes, and versioning metadata.
